MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Andy Katz, Esq. (510.465.4400; andy@andykatzlaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Matthew Yan, Esq. (415.554.3845; Matthew.Yan@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for disability discrimination, the third cause of action for failure to engage in the interactive process, and the fourth cause of action for failure to accommodate and granted as to the second cause of action for retaliation and the fifth cause of action for failure to prevent harassment and discrimination. Liberally construed, the declaration of Dr. Marvin Pietruszka provides sufficient admissible evidence to create a triable disputes on the discrimination and failure to accommodate claims. Liberally construed, the declaration of Latyshia Williams provides sufficient admissible evidence to create a triable dispute on the failure to engage in the interactive process claim. Plaintiff's sole asserted basis for the retaliation claim is that Mr. Williams accused the City of illegally terminating him is not supported by any evidence and thus the City is entitled to summary adjudication on that claim. Plaintiff has withdrawn the failure to prevent claim and thus the City is entitled to summary adjudication on that claim. The City's evidentiary objections 1 and 4 are sustained and objections 2,3 and 5 are overruled.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
